title: Add optional uWSGI field to Keystone CRD
summary: Changes correctly address gndrmnn's review #3 feedback: removed 'backward compatibility' comment from keystone_types.go, removed defensive clamping logic from uwsgiCommand(), removed corresponding unit tests, and deleted the review pattern file. All remaining tests pass and go vet is clean. One issue: CRD YAML description not regenerated after types.go comment change.

tests_checklist.item: All unit tests pass (go test ./... clean)
Removed tests correspond exactly to removed clamping behavior
Remaining uwsgi tests cover nil defaults, custom values, keepalive on/off, fixed flags
No regression in existing test suite

code_quality_checklist.item: Changes are minimal and focused on reviewer feedback

security_checklist.item: No security-relevant changes in this diff

architecture_checklist.item: uwsgiCommand still provides nil-safe defaults for existing CRs
Validation still enforced at CRD schema and webhook layers

dry_yagni_checklist.item: Removed unnecessary clamping per reviewer request

fail_fast_checklist.item: Invalid uwsgi values rejected at CRD schema and webhook validation layers

defensive_checklist.item: Webhook defense-in-depth validation for processes < 1 and threads < 1 still present

issues_found.description: CRD YAML description for spec.uwsgi still contains 'preserving backward compatibility' which was removed from keystone_types.go in this diff. The generated CRD manifest is stale — `make manifests` must be re-run to sync the CRD description with the updated Go comment.
issues_found.fix: Run `make manifests` in the operators/keystone directory to regenerate the CRD YAML from the updated types.go comments.
issues_found.evidence: CRD YAML line 677: 'httpKeepAlive=true) are used, preserving backward compatibility.' but keystone_types.go:109 now reads: '// httpKeepAlive=true) are used.'

suggested_improvements: keystone_webhook.go:85 contains 'uwsgiCommand applies a defense-in-depth clamp' which references the clamping logic removed in this diff — consider updating this comment to remove the stale reference
next_steps: Run `make manifests` to regenerate CRD YAML with updated description
